text.title: AI-Powered PM Platforms: 2026 Survey
text.summary: Comparison of AI-augmented PM platforms, their API agent-friendliness, and vendor lock-in risk for each AI feature.

rule.statement: Evaluate PM platforms on API agent-friendliness separately from UI AI features — UI AI features (Jira Rovo agents, ClickUp Autopilot, Monday Agent Factory) are not portable and not API-accessible.
rule.rationale: Vendor AI features create lock-in; Claude subagents operating via standard REST/GraphQL APIs remain portable across platform migrations. Choosing a platform for its AI UI features and then building automation on top of those features doubles the lock-in.

list.item: Jira + Rovo: AI work breakdown, issue creation from video/chat, workflow builder, Virtual Service Agent. Rovo agents not yet public API. Best for: Enterprise Agile, ITSM.
Monday.com: monday magic (prompt to solution), monday vibe (no-code apps), Agent Factory, predictive dashboards. GraphQL API v2 for standard CRUD. Best for: work management, CRM.
ClickUp Brain: neural network over tasks/docs/chat/calendar, ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini access, AI Scheduler, Autopilot Agents. AI features NOT API-accessible. Best for: all-in-one workspace.
Wrike: ML risk prediction (workload, overdue patterns). Risk prediction not API-exposed — only viewable in UI. Best for: enterprise risk-focused teams.
Linear: no native AI features, but best-in-class GraphQL API for agent automation. Best for: engineering teams, SDD-to-issue pipelines.
GitHub Issues + Projects: native CI/CD integration, REST + GraphQL API. Best for: engineering-only teams.

rule.statement: ClickUp AI Standard ($9/user/mo) covers unlimited essentials; AI Autopilot ($28/user/mo) required for unlimited agents and Enterprise Search — evaluate Autopilot tier only if agent automation replaces manual work worth more than the tier cost.
rule.rationale: Autopilot agents at $28/user cannot be cost-justified for teams where Claude subagents via the standard API deliver equivalent functionality.

section.title: Monday.com Results
section.p: Documented results: Pepsi reduced low-impact work 30% with monday magic; Five9 reduced time-to-revenue 25% with monday vibe no-code apps. Both use cases require dedicated Admin to configure and maintain AI workflows — account for ongoing governance cost.

antipattern.description: Building core PM automation on Jira Rovo agents, Monday Agent Factory, or ClickUp Autopilot without a migration path.
antipattern.reason: These are proprietary agent platforms not exportable to other tools; if the vendor changes pricing or deprecates the feature, the automation must be rebuilt from scratch on a new platform.
